title: Automate license, provenance, and not-medical-advice enforcement in CI
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual requires per-deliverable license and provenance verification as a hard gate. license-001 (M1) defines the checkable procedure. This task automates that procedure in CI so that every content deliverable is validated programmatically against the allow-list: missing attribution, provenance, or notMedicalAdvice, or a source-incompatible output license, all fail CI.
objective: Implement CI automation that validates each glossary entry and translation against the term-source allow-list, failing the build if: attribution is missing or incorrect, provenance is incomplete, notMedicalAdvice is not true, or the output license is source-incompatible.
acceptanceCriteria: A CI check script is added (tooling/check-license-provenance.js or wired into validate.ts) that, for each glossary entry and translation: verifies the source is in the allow-list with derivativesAllowed:true, confirms the required attribution string is present, checks provenance fields are complete, asserts notMedicalAdvice:true, and validates output license compatibility
CI fails hard (non-zero exit) if any of the above checks fails for any deliverable
The check is wired into pnpm test so it runs on every PR
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass on the PR
Implementation lives in the agent-neutral core (packages/schema or tooling/) per Elyos architecture rules, not in adapters/
output: PR adding automated license/provenance/notMedicalAdvice CI validation wired into pnpm test
